sources.title: Survodutide for the treatment of obesity: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, dose-finding phase 2 trial
sources.abstract: Phase 2 dose-ranging trial in adults with obesity. Survodutide 4.8mg achieved 18.7% weight loss at 46 weeks vs 2.1% placebo. Dose-dependent weight reduction with GI adverse events consistent with class.

sources.title: Survodutide for the Treatment of Participants with Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (MASH): A Phase 2 Randomized Clinical Trial
sources.abstract: Phase 2 trial in MASH with fibrosis (F1-F3). 83% achieved MASH resolution at 48 weeks with survodutide 6.0mg vs 18% placebo. Significant fibrosis improvement (64.5% vs 25.9%). Practice-changing MASH data.

sources.title: Efficacy and safety of BI 456906, a dual GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist, in type 2 diabetes: a randomized, double-blind, placebo and active-controlled, phase 2 trial
sources.abstract: Phase 2 in T2D. HbA1c reduction up to -1.9% vs placebo; weight loss up to -10% at 16 weeks. Superior to semaglutide 1mg for weight loss. First evidence of dual agonist efficacy in diabetes.

sources.title: BI 456906: A Novel, Dual Glucagon/GLP-1 Receptor Agonist with Potent Preclinical Activity for the Treatment of Obesity and Diabetes
sources.abstract: Preclinical characterization showing high affinity for both GLP-1R and GCGR. Mechanism studies demonstrate enhanced energy expenditure via glucagon component. Foundation for clinical development.

sources.title: First-in-human study of BI 456906, a dual glucagon/GLP-1 receptor agonist, shows safety and dose-dependent glucodynamic effects
sources.abstract: Phase 1 first-in-human study. Demonstrated safety profile, linear PK, ~6-7 day half-life supporting weekly dosing. Dose-dependent glucose lowering and weight loss observed.

sources.title: Glucagon/GLP-1 dual agonism for obesity and metabolic disease: current progress and future directions
sources.abstract: Comprehensive review of dual GLP-1/glucagon agonist mechanism and clinical development. Positions survodutide as lead candidate in class with unique liver-targeting benefits via glucagon component.

sources.title: Survodutide for the treatment of MASH: potential for liver-directed metabolic benefits
sources.abstract: Review of survodutide's hepatic mechanism of action. Glucagon receptor activation in liver drives enhanced lipid oxidation and anti-fibrotic effects. Explains superior MASH outcomes vs GLP-1-only agents.

sources.title: Glucagon receptor agonism: therapeutic target for metabolic liver disease
sources.abstract: Review of glucagon's hepatic effects. The liver lacks GLP-1R but is rich in GCGR, explaining why dual agonists like survodutide may have advantages for liver disease over GLP-1-only agonists.

sources.title: Dual GLP-1/glucagon agonists: systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical efficacy
sources.abstract: Meta-analysis of dual agonist trials including survodutide. Class demonstrates 15-20% weight loss with enhanced liver fat reduction compared to GLP-1-only agents. Confirms mechanism-based advantages.

sources.title: Body composition changes with survodutide: a phase 2 substudy analysis
sources.abstract: DEXA body composition substudy. Weight loss was 75% fat mass, 25% lean mass at 46 weeks. Glucagon component may enhance fat oxidation preferentially. Ratio similar to GLP-1 agonists.

sources.title: Glucagon receptor signaling in liver: therapeutic implications for NASH
sources.abstract: Mechanistic review of glucagon's role in hepatic metabolism. Supports dual agonist approach for NASH: glucagon drives lipid oxidation while GLP-1/GIP balance glycemia.

sources.title: The incretin effect and beyond: emerging roles of GLP-1 and glucagon
sources.abstract: Authoritative review by GLP-1 pioneer. Discusses synergy between GLP-1 and glucagon for metabolic disease. Theoretical foundation for dual agonist development.
